title: Shared Memory, Mutexes, Atomics, the Memory Model, and Race Evidence · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: Concurrent workers update shared counters, caches, and snapshots; plausible output hides races and broken publication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a race-free shared-state component while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting memory operations and establish sequenced-before, synchronized-before, and happens-before evidence.
Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.
Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
